name: Critical raw materials (REE, neon, helium, photoresist precursors)
description: The semi/datacenter materials base spans rare earth elements (NdPr for motor magnets in cooling/power, Dy/Tb for high-temp magnets), neon (excimer laser fill, mostly Ukrainian/Korean), helium (cryogenics + lithography), high-purity quartz (silicon crucibles), and photoresist precursors. Each is a sub-bottleneck with its own geopolitical exposure; collectively they do not currently gate the cycle but individually any one could.
stackPosition: Mine output (China, Australia, US-Mountain Pass, Malaysia-Lynas) → separation + refining (China 85%+) → metal/oxide forms → permanent magnet manufacture OR semiconductor process gas blends. The neon sub-chain: air-separation by-product (Ukraine + Korea + Russia historically; Korean expansion post-2022 invasion) → purification → mixed Ne/Xe/F gas blends → fab. Each material has a distinct supply chain; the common thread is concentration risk.

supplyStructure.name: China (REE refining)
supplyStructure.note: China Northern Rare Earth + China Rare Earth Group

supplyStructure.name: Ukraine + Korea (neon)
supplyStructure.note: Ukraine pre-war was ~40-50% of high-grade neon

supplyStructure.name: Sibelco (HPQ)
supplyStructure.note: Effective monopoly on highest-purity crucible quartz

indicators.name: REE price index — NdPr, Dy, Tb spot
MP Materials and Lynas operating results + concentrate / oxide shipments
Neon price commentary in fab earnings (SK Hynix, Samsung Foundry)
China export-control announcements on REE / processing tech
US Defense Production Act / DOE critical materials investments

companies.name: MP Materials
companies.note: Only US-listed integrated REE play; Mountain Pass + Fort Worth processing

companies.name: Sibelco
companies.note: Spruce Pine HPQ monopoly — only commercial source of HPQ-1 globally

companies.name: USA Rare Earth
companies.note: Round Top heavy-REE deposit + Stillwater OK magnet plant; DoD-backed
companies.funding: SPAC merger pending (Inflection Point Acquisition Corp II)

companies.name: Niron Magnetics
companies.note: Rare-earth-free iron-nitride magnets; alternative path around China REE controls
companies.investors: GM Ventures, Stellantis Ventures, Volvo Cars

companies.name: Air Products / Linde
companies.note: Industrial gases including neon, helium; AI exposure dilutes in vast diversified businesses

companies.name: Tokuyama / Shin-Etsu / Mitsubishi Chemical
companies.note: Photoresist precursors; concentrated Japanese supply chain

insight: Raw materials are the tail-risk bottleneck — none is currently gating the cycle, but several have asymmetric downside if geopolitics break the wrong way. The watch items in order of severity: (1) China REE export-controls extending to refined NdPr / Dy, which would whack permanent-magnet motor supply across cooling and EV; (2) Sibelco Spruce Pine HPQ — a single North Carolina quarry feeds essentially every silicon wafer crucible globally, with Hurricane Helene proving the single-point-of-failure; (3) neon back to spike if Korea expansion underdelivers. The investable wedge is MP Materials at any geopolitical re-rating, plus a long-dated option on whoever owns Sibelco when it eventually surfaces. None of this is an AI-cycle thesis on its own — it's a portfolio-protection thesis layered behind the cycle.

debates.question: Does China impose new export controls on refined REE / magnets by end of 2026?
debates.currentBelief: Likely on at least one heavy-REE or magnet category; precise scope uncertain

debates.question: Does a credible second-source HPQ-1 emerge by 2028?
debates.currentBelief: Norwegian / Australian projects exist but qualification is multi-year; unlikely at scale by 2028

debates.question: Does neon pricing re-spike (>5× current) at any point through 2027?
debates.currentBelief: Possible if Korean expansion underdelivers OR Russia-side supply normalizes faster than consensus; both look ~10% likely
